explanation: Transit-related venues saw a notable increase in visitation, with Train Stations rising from 9% to 11% and Airports emerging as a top category at 3% in 2018.
The most notable change is the emergence of Train Stations as the third most visited category in 2018 at 5% (up from less than 2% in 2013), showing a clear trend toward transit-oriented activity.

Between 2013 and 2018, visitation to transit options like train stations dropped significantly, while food and beverage categories such as Cafés and Coffee Shops saw increases in their shares, alongside the emergence of Markets in the top category mix.

There is a prominent increase in visits to outdoor public spaces, with plazas and parks rising to become the most visited categories in 2018, while routine transit, work, and university locations declined.
Between 2013 and 2018, there is a clear emergence of gym and fitness-related categories in the top mix, rising to a combined 5%, while work (Office) and residential (Home) visits decreased.
The visitation share for food and beverage categories saw a notable increase, with Cafés rising from 8% to 10% and Coffee Shops entering the top categories at 5% in 2018.
Visitation shifted dramatically from private homes toward food and beverage venues, with Cafés and Coffee Shops rising from a combined 6% in 2013 to 27% of the total category mix in 2018.
The share of visits to shopping malls saw a notable increase from 21% in 2013 to 25% in 2018, making it the most prominent change in the category mix.

Between 2013 and 2018, transit-related visitations (Metro, Train, Road) saw a major decline, while food and beverage categories expanded with the emergence of Bar (3%) and Coffee Shop (2%) alongside Bakery (2%).
